question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked no didactic questions. It explicitly noted 'No propose_options tool available' and proceeded directly to implementation with inline teaching notes. The closing 'Want to run the official Exercism test suite...?' is a logistics question, not a teachable-moment prompt.

question_quality.rationale: No substantive choices were surfaced to the user. The implementation decisions (translate vs dict, validation strategy, module-level constants) were all made unilaterally and explained post-hoc rather than framed as forks.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolded sequence — the Pair wrote the full solution in one shot. The 'teaching notes' are a monologue lecture, not a progression of graduated questions.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the explanations are clear and accurate, but there is essentially no back-and-forth — the user only echoed a summary. Conversational flow is one-sided.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is functionally equivalent to the baseline: same `str.maketrans`/`translate` approach, same frozenset validation, ValueError on bad input, all assertions pass. Minor cosmetic differences in error formatting.

friction.rationale: Completed in 39s vs baseline's 70s with comparable output and added (one-sided) teaching commentary. Tighter than baseline, though without genuine pedagogical interaction the extra context is mostly explanatory prose.
